commit.message:
Fix NCAAB edge inflation — recalibrate blend weights and min_edge

Problem: Pythagorean model (exponent 11.5) caps win prob at 0.95 for many
matchups. With 40% model weight in blend, 73/85 games were showing 14-19%
edge against retail lines — clearly over-inflated.

Fixes:
- Blend: 60/40 Pinnacle/model → 80/20 (Pinnacle is sharper than any CBB model)
- Cap model_home_prob at [0.30, 0.70] before blending to prevent extreme
  Pythagorean outputs from dominating the signal
- Raise min_edge gate: 2.5% → 5% (pre-portfolio filter AND optimizer threshold)

Result: 73 qualifying plays → 16 qualifying plays
        Edge range: 14-19% → 2.8-5.2% (realistic vs retail juice)

Tests: 248 passed, 0 failed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fixes #4766 - Implement `DropDownList` control (#4783)

* Initial plan

* Add EditDropDown control implementation

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add EditDropDown scenario example and unit tests

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Popovers.md documentation to mention EditDropDown

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename EditDropDown to DropDownList

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor DropDownList; add popover mouse dismiss tests

Modernize DropDownList control with improved initialization, popover management, and event handling. Use object initializers and concise lambdas in example code. Remove obsolete handlers and clarify type usage. Add comprehensive PopoverMouseDismissTests to verify popover dismissal logic and event recursion. Improves code clarity, robustness, and test coverage.

Prevent popover re-show on dismiss; add mouse tests

- Add DismissedByMousePress guard to MouseImpl to prevent re-entrant popover show during the same mouse click cycle
- Update ApplicationPopover.Show to respect the guard, blocking accidental re-opening of popovers by views beneath them
- Add extensive unit tests for popover mouse dismiss logic, including recursion, event handling, and guard behavior
- Refactor DropDownList to use new popover guard and modern event syntax
- Improve documentation on popover dismiss/re-show prevention and best practices
- Minor code cleanups and formatting improvements

* Improve DropDownList UX and fix ViewportToScreen sizing

- Enhanced DropDownList usability: clearer help text, better layout, and visual feedback in ReadOnly mode.
- Improved ListView popover styling and ensured color scheme consistency.
- Added mouse activation and design-time support to DropDownList.
- Changed View.ViewportToScreen() to return Viewport size, not Frame size; updated related tests.

commit.message:
Add timeout to prevent verifier hang on malformed programs (#763)

* Add timeout mechanism to prevent verifier infinite loops

Add a 5-second timeout around prevail::analyze() to prevent the fuzzer
from hanging on malformed BPF programs that cause the verifier to enter
infinite loops (e.g., nested loops which are a known limitation of prevail).

The timeout uses a separate thread with std::future::wait_for() to detect
when verification takes too long. If the timeout is reached, the verification
thread is detached and the function returns false (verification failed).

This fix addresses CI failures where the fuzzer would timeout after 60+
seconds on programs that trigger verifier infinite loops.

Co-authored-by: Alan-Jowett <20480683+Alan-Jowett@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update documentation with implementation notes

Co-authored-by: Alan-Jowett <20480683+Alan-Jowett@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Update documentation with implementation notes"

This reverts commit e1c27a141df89e117cf70058302a1d4a4d4e055f.

* Move verification state to heap to fix use-after-free on timeout

Address review feedback:
- Move all shared state (program, result, promise, completion flag) to
  heap-allocated shared_ptrs so detached thread cannot access destroyed
  stack locals on timeout.
- Add safe_set_value wrapper to guard against set_value throwing when
  promise is already satisfied or has no state.

- Add CancellationToken property to VerificationOptions

- Check cancellation between function verifications in ContractVerificationPass
- Pass cancellationToken from all 14 MCP tools that call Program.Compile()

verifications between contract checks, preventing server hangs.

* feat(auth): embed schoolID in refresh token JWT to preserve school context

Add SchoolID field to Claims and update GenerateMinimalToken to accept
a schoolID parameter. This ensures the school context selected by the
user (including via switchContext) survives token rotation.

Previously, on every access token refresh the backend rebuilt the
context using the user's first membership school, losing the school
the user had explicitly selected. Now the refresh JWT carries the
schoolID and the backend uses it to reconstruct the correct context.

Backward compatible: tokens without schoolID fall back to firstSchoolID
from memberships (existing behavior for legacy tokens).
